lines.text: The image-only multifamily experiment evaluates target-level boxes on 61 matched rows. The clearest image-only diagnostic signal is the space/room branch after mask-to-box repair. It obtains precision 0.274181, recall 0.
- Do not claim: full raster scene-graph solved.
Status: **bounded draft for internal review; metrics below 0.95 are treated as diagnostic/operating-point evidence, not final high-accuracy claims**

This experiment supports a bounded operating-point claim: named opt-in symbol policies can be evaluated for precision/inflation trade-offs, without implying high-accuracy symbol recognition or changing runtime defaults s

The main result of this work is not a high-accuracy monolithic detector for raster floorplan understanding. Instead, the contribution is an audit-gated mixture-of-experts framework that makes floorplan parsing decisions

The public-raster symbol results demonstrate the value of audited policy handoff, but not high-accuracy symbol recognition. The precision-oriented opt-in policy improves target-level symbol F1 and reduces prediction infl

Most visual detection metrics in this draft are below 0.95. They are therefore treated as operating-point, ablation, diagnostic, or negative-evidence results rather than near-perfect recognition claims. The only paper-fa

Raster floorplan understanding remains difficult because architectural symbols, room regions, text, and relations have different visual scales and failure modes. We present an audit-gated mixture-of-experts pipeline for

key_changes: Inserted explicit Sub-0.95 Metric Policy section.
Reframed p100 symbol as operating-point/inflation reduction, not high-accuracy recognition.
Reframed image-only room/space as measurable diagnostic signal, not solved extraction.
Kept P095 positive only as expected-json replay.
Preserved negative evidence language for symbol/text and selector probes.
